tEn: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
tZh: Toolformer：语言模型可以自学使用工具
sEn: Language models can teach themselves to use external tools via simple APIs, achieving better performance without sacrificing core language modeling abilities.
sZh: 语言模型可以通过简单的API自学使用外部工具，在提升性能的同时不牺牲核心语言建模能力。
absEn: Language models (LMs) exhibit remarkable abilities to solve new tasks from just a few examples or textual instructions, especially at scale. They also, paradoxically, struggle with basic functionality, such as arithmetic or factual lookup, where much simpler and smaller models excel. In this paper, we show that LMs can teach themselves to use external tools via simple APIs and achieve the best of both worlds. We introduce Toolformer, a model trained to decide which APIs to call, when to call them, what arguments to pass, and how to best incorporate the results into future token prediction. This is done in a self-supervised way, requiring nothing more than a handful of demonstrations for each API. We incorporate a range of tools, including a calculator, a Q\&A system, two different search engines, a translation system, and a calendar. Toolformer achieves substantially improved zero-shot performance across a variety of downstream tasks, often competitive with much larger models, without sacrificing its core language modeling abilities.
absZh: 语言模型（LM）在仅凭少量示例或文本指令解决新任务方面表现出色，尤其是在规模扩大时。然而，它们却在算术或事实查找等基本功能上表现不佳，而这些功能更简单、更小的模型却擅长。在本文中，我们展示了LM可以通过简单的API自学使用外部工具，从而两全其美。我们引入了Toolformer，这是一个经过训练的模型，能够决定调用哪些API、何时调用、传递什么参数以及如何最好地将结果整合到未来的令牌预测中。这是以自监督方式完成的，每个API仅需少量演示。我们整合了一系列工具，包括计算器、问答系统、两种不同的搜索引擎、翻译系统和日历。Toolformer在多种下游任务中实现了显著改进的零样本性能，通常与更大的模型竞争，且不牺牲其核心语言建模能力。

contrib.en: Proposes Toolformer, a self-supervised method for LMs to learn when and how to use external tools via API calls.
contrib.zh: 提出Toolformer，一种自监督方法，让语言模型学习何时以及如何通过API调用使用外部工具。

contrib.en: Integrates multiple tools (calculator, QA system, search engine, translation, calendar) without task-specific supervision.
contrib.zh: 集成多种工具（计算器、问答系统、搜索引擎、翻译、日历），无需任务特定监督。

contrib.en: Uses perplexity-based filtering to select helpful API calls from LM-generated candidates.
contrib.zh: 使用基于困惑度的过滤方法，从语言模型生成的候选中选择有用的API调用。

contrib.en: Achieves strong zero-shot performance on arithmetic, factual lookup, QA, and temporal tasks, often beating much larger models.
contrib.zh: 在算术、事实查找、问答和时间任务上取得强大的零样本性能，常超越更大模型。

contrib.en: Preserves core language modeling abilities, as perplexity on standard benchmarks remains unaffected.
contrib.zh: 保留核心语言建模能力，标准基准上的困惑度不受影响。

contrib.en: Demonstrates scaling benefits: larger LMs benefit more from learning tool use.
contrib.zh: 展示规模扩张的好处：更大的语言模型从学习工具使用中获益更多。

limits.en: Cannot chain multiple API calls; each call is generated independently.
limits.zh: 无法链式调用多个API；每个调用独立生成。

limits.en: Lacks interactive tool use, e.g., refining search queries or browsing results.
limits.zh: 缺乏交互式工具使用，例如优化搜索查询或浏览结果。

limits.en: Sensitive to input phrasing when deciding whether to call an API.
limits.zh: 在决定是否调用API时对输入措辞敏感。

limits.en: Sample-inefficient for some tools; e.g., calculator yields few useful calls from millions of documents.
limits.zh: 某些工具样本效率低；例如，计算器从数百万文档中仅产生少量有用调用。

limits.en: Does not account for computational cost of API calls during decision-making.
limits.zh: 决策时不考虑API调用的计算成本。

items.en: Language models (LMs) exhibit remarkable abilities to solve new tasks from just a few examples or textual instructions, especially at scale. They also, paradoxically, struggle with basic functionality, such as arithmetic or factual lookup, where much simpler and smaller models excel. In this paper, we show that LMs can teach themselves to use external tools via simple APIs and achieve the best of both worlds. We introduce Toolformer, a model trained to decide which APIs to call, when to call them, what arguments to pass, and how to best incorporate the results into future token prediction. This is done in a self-supervised way, requiring nothing more than a handful of demonstrations for each API. We incorporate a range of tools, including a calculator, a Q&A system, a search engine, a translation system, and a calendar. Toolformer achieves substantially improved zero-shot performance across a variety of downstream tasks, often competitive with much larger models, without sacrificing its core language modeling abilities.
items.zh: 语言模型（LM）展现出从少量示例或文本指令中解决新任务的非凡能力，尤其是在规模较大时。然而，矛盾的是，它们在算术或事实查询等基本功能上却表现挣扎，而这些任务中更简单、更小的模型却表现出色。在本文中，我们展示了语言模型可以通过简单的API自学使用外部工具，从而实现两全其美。我们引入了Toolformer，这是一个经过训练的模型，能够决定调用哪些API、何时调用、传递什么参数，以及如何最好地将结果整合到未来的词预测中。这一切都是以自监督方式完成的，每个API仅需少量演示。我们整合了一系列工具，包括计算器、问答系统、搜索引擎、翻译系统和日历。Toolformer在多种下游任务中实现了显著改进的零样本性能，通常与更大的模型竞争，且不牺牲其核心语言建模能力。

items.en: Large language models achieve impressive zero- and few-shot results on a variety of natural language processing tasks (Brown et al., 2020; Chowdhery et al., 2022, i.a.) and show several emergent capabilities (Wei et al., 2022). However, all of these models have several inherent limitations that can at best be partially addressed by further scaling. These limitations include an inability to access up-to-date information on recent events (Komeili et al., 2022) and the related tendency to hallucinate facts (Maynez et al., 2020; Ji et al., 2022), difficulties in understanding low-resource languages (Lin et al., 2021), a lack of mathematical skills to perform precise calculations (Patel et al., 2021) and an unawareness of the progression of time (Dhingra et al., 2022).
items.zh: 大型语言模型在各种自然语言处理任务上取得了令人印象深刻的零样本和少样本结果（Brown等人，2020；Chowdhery等人，2022等），并展现出若干涌现能力（Wei等人，2022）。然而，所有这些模型都有几个固有的局限性，这些局限性最多只能通过进一步扩展来部分解决。这些局限性包括无法访问最新事件的最新信息（Komeili等人，2022）以及相关的事实幻觉倾向（Maynez等人，2020；Ji等人，2022），理解低资源语言的困难（Lin等人，2021），缺乏进行精确计算的数学技能（Patel等人，2021），以及对时间流逝的无知（Dhingra等人，2022）。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Exemplary predictions of Toolformer. The model autonomously decides to call different APIs (from top to bottom: a question answering system, a calculator, a machine translation system, and a Wikipedia search engine) to obtain information that is useful for completing a piece of text.
items.capZh: 图1：Toolformer的示例预测。模型自主决定调用不同的API（从上到下：问答系统、计算器、机器翻译系统和维基百科搜索引擎）以获取有助于完成文本片段的信息。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Key steps in our approach, illustrated for a question answering tool: Given an input text 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x}, we first sample a position i𝑖i and corresponding API call candidates ci1,ci2,…,ciksuperscriptsubscript𝑐𝑖1superscriptsubscript𝑐𝑖2…superscriptsubscript𝑐𝑖𝑘c_{i}^{1},c_{i}^{2},\ldots,c_{i}^{k}. We then execute these API calls and filter out all calls which do not reduce the loss Lisubscript𝐿𝑖L_{i} over the next tokens. All remaining API calls are interleaved with the original text, resulting in a new text 𝐱∗superscript𝐱\mathbf{x}^{*}.
items.capZh: 图2：我们方法的关键步骤，以问答工具为例：给定输入文本𝐱，我们首先采样一个位置i和相应的API调用候选c_i^1, c_i^2, …, c_i^k。然后执行这些API调用，并过滤掉所有不能降低后续词损失L_i的调用。所有剩余的API调用与原始文本交错，形成新文本𝐱*。

items.en: A simple way to overcome these limitations of today’s language models is to give them the ability to use external tools such as search engines, calculators, or calendars. However, existing approaches either rely on large amounts of human annotations (Komeili et al., 2022; Thoppilan et al., 2022) or limit tool use to task-specific settings only (e.g., Gao et al., 2022; Parisi et al., 2022), hindering a more widespread adoption of tool use in LMs. Therefore, we propose Toolformer, a model that learns to use tools in a novel way, which fulfills the following desiderata:
items.zh: 克服当今语言模型这些局限性的一个简单方法是赋予它们使用外部工具的能力，例如搜索引擎、计算器或日历。然而，现有方法要么依赖大量人工标注（Komeili等人，2022；Thoppilan等人，2022），要么将工具使用限制在特定任务设置中（例如，Gao等人，2022；Parisi等人，2022），阻碍了工具使用在语言模型中的更广泛采用。因此，我们提出Toolformer，一种以新颖方式学习使用工具的模型，满足以下期望：

items.en: The use of tools should be learned in a self-supervised way without requiring large amounts of human annotations. This is important not only because of the costs associated with such annotations, but also because what humans find useful may be different from what a model finds useful.
items.zh: 工具的使用应以自监督方式学习，无需大量人工标注。这一点很重要，不仅因为此类标注的成本，还因为人类认为有用的东西可能与模型认为有用的不同。

items.en: The LM should not lose any of its generality and should be able to decide for itself when and how to use which tool. In contrast to existing approaches, this enables a much more comprehensive use of tools that is not tied to specific tasks.
items.zh: 语言模型不应失去其通用性，并且应能自行决定何时以及如何使用哪种工具。与现有方法相比，这使得工具的使用更加全面，不局限于特定任务。

items.en: Our approach for achieving these goals is based on the recent idea of using large LMs with in-context learning (Brown et al., 2020) to generate entire datasets from scratch (Schick and Schütze, 2021b; Honovich et al., 2022; Wang et al., 2022): Given just a handful of human-written examples of how an API can be used, we let a LM annotate a huge language modeling dataset with potential API calls. We then use a self-supervised loss to determine which of these API calls actually help the model in predicting future tokens. Finally, we finetune the LM itself on the API calls that it considers useful. As illustrated in Figure 1, through this simple approach, LMs can learn to control a variety of tools, and to choose for themselves which tool to use when and how.
items.zh: 我们实现这些目标的方法基于最近的想法，即使用具有上下文学习能力的大型语言模型（Brown等人，2020）从头生成整个数据集（Schick和Schütze，2021b；Honovich等人，2022；Wang等人，2022）：给定仅少量人工编写的API使用示例，我们让语言模型用潜在的API调用注释一个大型语言建模数据集。然后，我们使用自监督损失来确定哪些API调用实际上有助于模型预测未来词。最后，我们在模型认为有用的API调用上微调语言模型本身。如图1所示，通过这种简单方法，语言模型可以学会控制多种工具，并自行选择何时以及如何使用哪种工具。

items.en: As our approach is agnostic of the dataset being used, we can apply it to the exact same dataset that was used to pretrain a model in the first place. This ensures that the model does not lose any of its generality and language modeling abilities. We conduct experiments on a variety of different downstream tasks, demonstrating that after learning to use tools, Toolformer, which is based on a pretrained GPT-J model (Wang and Komatsuzaki, 2021) with 6.7B parameters, achieves much stronger zero-shot results, clearly outperforming a much larger GPT-3 model (Brown et al., 2020) and several other baselines on various tasks.
items.zh: 由于我们的方法与所使用的数据集无关，我们可以将其应用于最初用于预训练模型的完全相同的数据集。这确保了模型不会失去其任何通用性和语言建模能力。我们在各种不同的下游任务上进行了实验，结果表明，在学习使用工具后，基于预训练GPT-J模型（Wang和Komatsuzaki，2021，6.7B参数）的Toolformer在零样本结果上取得了显著提升，明显优于更大的GPT-3模型（Brown等人，2020）以及多个其他基线。

items.en: Our aim is to equip a language model M𝑀M with the ability to use different tools by means of API calls. We require that inputs and outputs for each API can be represented as text sequences. This allows seamless insertion of API calls into any given text, using special tokens to mark the start and end of each such call.
items.zh: 我们的目标是通过API调用赋予语言模型M使用不同工具的能力。我们要求每个API的输入和输出都可以表示为文本序列。这允许将API调用无缝插入任何给定文本中，使用特殊标记标记每个此类调用的开始和结束。

Given an API call c𝑐c with a corresponding result r𝑟r, we denote the linearized sequences of the API call not including and including its result, respectively, as:

items.en: where “<API>”, “</API>” and “→→\rightarrow” are special tokens.111In practice, we use the token sequences “ [”, “]” and “->” to represent “<API>”, “</API>” and “→→\rightarrow”, respectively. This enables our approach to work without modifying the existing LM’s vocabulary. For reasons of readability, we still refer to them as “<API>”, “</API>” and “→→\rightarrow” throughout this section. Some examples of linearized API calls inserted into text sequences are shown in Figure 1.

items.en: Given a dataset 𝒞={𝐱1,…,𝐱|𝒞|}𝒞superscript𝐱1…superscript𝐱𝒞\mathcal{C}=\{\mathbf{x}^{1},\ldots,\mathbf{x}^{|\mathcal{C}|}\} of plain texts, we first convert this dataset into a dataset 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} augmented with API calls. This is done in three steps, illustrated in Figure 2: First, we exploit the in-context learning ability of M𝑀M to sample a large number of potential API calls. We then execute these API calls and finally check whether the obtained responses are helpful for predicting future tokens; this is used as a filtering criterion. After filtering, we merge API calls for different tools, resulting in the augmented dataset 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*}, and finetune M𝑀M itself on this dataset. Each of these steps is described in more detail below.

items.en: As a next step, we execute all API calls generated by M𝑀M to obtain the corresponding results. How this is done depends entirely on the API itself – for example, it can involve calling another neural network, executing a Python script or using a retrieval system to perform search over a large corpus. The response for each API call cisubscript𝑐𝑖c_{i} needs to be a single text sequence risubscript𝑟𝑖r_{i}.
items.zh: 下一步，我们执行M生成的所有API调用以获得相应结果。如何执行完全取决于API本身——例如，可以涉及调用另一个神经网络、执行Python脚本或使用检索系统在大规模语料库上进行搜索。每个API调用c_i的响应需要是一个单一的文本序列r_i。

The former is the weighted loss over all tokens xi,…,xnsubscript𝑥𝑖…subscript𝑥𝑛x_{i},\ldots,x_{n} if the API call and its result are given to M𝑀M as a prefix;333We provide e​(ci,ri)esubscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑟𝑖\text{e}(c_{i},r_{i}) as a prefix instead of inserting it at position i𝑖i because M𝑀M is not yet finetuned on any examples containing API calls, so inserting it in the middle of 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x} would interrupt the flow and not align with patterns in the pretraining corpus, thus hurting perplexity. the latter is the minimum of the losses obtained from (i) doing no API call at all and (ii) doing an API call, but not providing the response. Intuitively, an API call is helpful to M𝑀M if providing it with both the input and the output of this call makes it easier for the model to predict future tokens, compared to not receiving the API call at all, or receiving only its input. Given a filtering threshold τfsubscript𝜏𝑓\tau_{f}, we thus only keep API calls for which
items.zh: 其中 ε 表示空序列。前者是当 API 调用及其结果作为前缀提供给 M 时，对所有词元 xi,…,xn 的加权损失；333我们将 e(ci,ri) 作为前缀提供，而不是将其插入到位置 i，因为 M 尚未在任何包含 API 调用的示例上进行微调，因此将其插入到 x 中间会中断流程，并且与预训练语料库中的模式不一致，从而损害困惑度。后者是以下两种损失的最小值：(i) 完全不进行 API 调用，以及 (ii) 进行 API 调用但不提供响应。直观地说，如果同时提供 API 调用的输入和输出，使得模型预测未来词元比完全不接收 API 调用或仅接收其输入更容易，那么 API 调用对 M 是有帮助的。给定过滤阈值 τf，我们只保留满足以下条件的 API 调用：

items.en: holds, i.e., adding the API call and its result reduces the loss by at least τfsubscript𝜏𝑓\tau_{f}, compared to not doing any API call or obtaining no result from it.
items.zh: 即，与不进行任何 API 调用或未从其获得结果相比，添加 API 调用及其结果至少将损失减少 τf。

items.en: After sampling and filtering calls for all APIs, we finally merge the remaining API calls and interleave them with the original inputs. That is, for an input text 𝐱=x1,…,xn𝐱subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑛\mathbf{x}=x_{1},\ldots,x_{n} with a corresponding API call and result (ci,ri)subscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑟𝑖(c_{i},r_{i}) at position i𝑖i, we construct the new sequence 𝐱∗=x1:i−1,e​(ci,ri),xi:nsuperscript𝐱subscript𝑥:1𝑖1esubscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑟𝑖subscript𝑥:𝑖𝑛\mathbf{x}^{*}=x_{1:{i-1}},\text{e}(c_{i},r_{i}),x_{i:n}; we proceed analogously for texts with multiple API calls. Doing this for all 𝐱∈𝒞𝐱𝒞\mathbf{x}\in\mathcal{C} results in the new dataset 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} augmented with API calls. We use this new dataset to finetune M𝑀M, using a standard language modeling objective. Crucially, apart from inserted API calls the augmented dataset 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} contains the exact same texts as 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C}, the original dataset. As a consequence, finetuning M𝑀M on 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} exposes it to the same content as finetuning on 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C}. Moreover, as API calls are inserted in exactly those positions and with exactly those inputs that help M𝑀M predict future tokens, finetuning on 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} enables the language model to decide when and how to use which tool, based purely on its own feedback.
items.zh: 在为所有 API 采样和过滤调用后，我们最终合并剩余的 API 调用，并将它们与原始输入交错排列。也就是说，对于输入文本 x=x1,…,xn，在位置 i 处有相应的 API 调用和结果 (ci,ri)，我们构造新序列 x∗=x1:i-1,e(ci,ri),xi:n；对于具有多个 API 调用的文本，我们类似地处理。对所有 x∈C 执行此操作，得到用 API 调用增强的新数据集 C∗。我们使用这个新数据集，通过标准的语言建模目标来微调 M。关键的是，除了插入的 API 调用之外，增强数据集 C∗ 包含与原始数据集 C 完全相同的文本。因此，在 C∗ 上微调 M 使其暴露于与在 C 上微调相同的内容。此外，由于 API 调用被精确地插入在那些位置，并且使用那些恰好帮助 M 预测未来词元的输入，因此在 C∗ 上微调使语言模型能够纯粹基于其自身的反馈来决定何时以及如何使用哪个工具。

items.en: When generating text with M𝑀M after finetuning with our approach, we perform regular decoding until M𝑀M produces the “→→\rightarrow” token, indicating that it next expects the response for an API call. At this point, we interrupt the decoding process, call the appropriate API to get a response, and continue the decoding process after inserting both the response and the </API> token.
items.zh: 在使用我们的方法微调后，当用 M 生成文本时，我们执行常规解码，直到 M 生成“→”词元，表示它接下来期望 API 调用的响应。此时，我们中断解码过程，调用相应的 API 获取响应，并在插入响应和 </API> 词元后继续解码过程。

items.en: We explore a variety of tools to address different shortcomings of regular LMs. The only constraints we impose on these tools is that (i) both their inputs and outputs can be represented as text sequences, and (ii) we can obtain a few demonstrations of their intended use. Concretely, we explore the following five tools: a question answering system, a Wikipedia search engine, a calculator, a calendar, and a machine translation system. Some examples of potential calls and return strings for the APIs associated with each of these tools are shown in Table 1. We briefly discuss all tools below; further details can be found in Appendix A.
items.zh: 我们探索了各种工具来解决常规 LM 的不同缺陷。我们对这些工具施加的唯一约束是：(i) 它们的输入和输出都可以表示为文本序列，以及 (ii) 我们可以获得一些关于它们预期用途的演示。具体来说，我们探索了以下五种工具：问答系统、维基百科搜索引擎、计算器、日历和机器翻译系统。表 1 显示了与每个工具相关的 API 的一些潜在调用和返回字符串示例。我们简要讨论所有工具；更多细节可在附录 A 中找到。

items.en: Our first tool is a question answering system based on another LM that can answer simple factoid questions. Specifically, we use Atlas (Izacard et al., 2022), a retrieval-augmented LM finetuned on Natural Questions (Kwiatkowski et al., 2019).
items.zh: 我们的第一个工具是基于另一个 LM 的问答系统，可以回答简单的事实性问题。具体来说，我们使用 Atlas（Izacard 等人，2022），这是一个在 Natural Questions（Kwiatkowski 等人，2019）上微调的检索增强 LM。

items.en: As a second tool, we use a calculator that can perform simple numeric calculations; we only support the four basic arithmetic operations. Results are always rounded to two decimal places.
items.zh: 作为第二个工具，我们使用一个可以执行简单数值计算的计算器；我们只支持四种基本算术运算。结果总是四舍五入到两位小数。

items.en: Our third tool is a search engine that, given a search term, returns short text snippets from Wikipedia. Compared to our question answering tool, this search enables a model to get more comprehensive information on a subject, but requires it to extract the relevant parts by itself. As our search engine, we use a BM25 retriever (Robertson et al., 1995; Baeza-Yates et al., 1999) that indexes the Wikipedia dump from KILT (Petroni et al., 2021).
items.zh: 我们的第三个工具是一个搜索引擎，给定搜索词，返回来自维基百科的简短文本片段。与我们的问答工具相比，这种搜索使模型能够获得关于某个主题的更全面信息，但需要它自己提取相关部分。作为我们的搜索引擎，我们使用 BM25 检索器（Robertson 等人，1995；Baeza-Yates 等人，1999），它索引来自 KILT（Petroni 等人，2021）的维基百科转储。

items.en: Our fourth tool is a machine translation system based on a LM that can translate a phrase from any language into English. More concretely, we use the 600M parameter NLLB (Costa-jussà et al., 2022) as our multilingual machine translation model that works for 200 languages (including low-resource ones). The source language is automatically detected using the fastText classifier (Joulin et al., 2016), while the target language is always set to English.
items.zh: 我们的第四个工具是一个基于 LM 的机器翻译系统，可以将任何语言的短语翻译成英语。更具体地说，我们使用 600M 参数的 NLLB（Costa-jussà 等人，2022）作为我们的多语言机器翻译模型，适用于 200 种语言（包括低资源语言）。源语言使用 fastText 分类器（Joulin 等人，2016）自动检测，而目标语言始终设置为英语。

items.en: Our final tool is a calendar API that, when queried, returns the current date without taking any input. This provides temporal context for predictions that require some awareness of time.
items.zh: 我们的最后一个工具是一个日历 API，当被查询时，返回当前日期而不接受任何输入。这为需要一定时间意识的预测提供了时间上下文。

items.en: We investigate whether our approach enables a model to use tools without any further supervision and to decide for itself when and how to call which of the available tools. To test this, we select a variety of downstream tasks where we assume at least one of the considered tools to be useful, and evaluate performance in zero-shot settings (Section 4.2). Beyond that, we also ensure that our approach does not hurt the model’s core language modeling abilities; we verify this by looking at perplexity on two language modeling datasets (Section 4.3). Finally, we investigate how the ability to learn using tools is affected by model size (Section 4.4).
items.zh: 我们研究我们的方法是否能使模型在没有任何进一步监督的情况下使用工具，并自行决定何时以及如何调用哪些可用工具。为了测试这一点，我们选择了各种下游任务，假设其中至少有一个考虑的工具是有用的，并在零样本设置中评估性能（第 4.2 节）。除此之外，我们还确保我们的方法不会损害模型的核心语言建模能力；我们通过查看两个语言建模数据集上的困惑度来验证这一点（第 4.3 节）。最后，我们研究了使用工具学习的能力如何受模型大小的影响（第 4.4 节）。

items.en: Throughout all of our experiments, we use a subset of CCNet (Wenzek et al., 2020) as our language modeling dataset 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C} and GPT-J (Wang and Komatsuzaki, 2021) as our language model M𝑀M. To reduce the computational cost of annotating 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C} with API calls, we define heuristics for some APIs to get a subset of 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C} for which API calls are more likely to be helpful than for an average text. For example, we only consider texts for the calculator tool if they contain at least three numbers. Details of the heuristics used are given in Appendix A. For obtaining 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} from 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C}, we perform all steps described in Section 2 and additionally filter out all examples for which all API calls were eliminated in the filtering step.444While this filtering alters the distribution of training examples, we assume that the remaining examples are close enough to the original distribution so that M𝑀M’s language modeling abilities remain unaffected. This assumption is empirically validated in Section 4.3. For the weighting function, we use
items.zh: 在我们所有的实验中，我们使用 CCNet（Wenzek 等人，2020）的一个子集作为我们的语言建模数据集 C，并使用 GPT-J（Wang 和 Komatsuzaki，2021）作为我们的语言模型 M。为了降低用 API 调用注释 C 的计算成本，我们为某些 API 定义了启发式规则，以获取 C 的一个子集，其中 API 调用比平均文本更可能有用。例如，我们只考虑包含至少三个数字的文本用于计算器工具。使用的启发式规则细节在附录 A 中给出。为了从 C 获得 C∗，我们执行第 2 节中描述的所有步骤，并额外过滤掉所有 API 调用在过滤步骤中被消除的示例。444虽然这种过滤改变了训练示例的分布，但我们假设剩余的示例足够接近原始分布，以至于 M 的语言建模能力不受影响。这一假设在第 4.3 节中得到了经验验证。对于加权函数，我们使用

items.en: to make sure that API calls happen close to where the information provided by the API is actually helpful for the model. The thresholds τssubscript𝜏𝑠\tau_{s} and τfsubscript𝜏𝑓\tau_{f} are chosen individually for each tool to ensure a sufficiently larger number of examples; see Appendix A for details. Table 2 shows relevant statistics of our final dataset augmented with API calls.
items.zh: 以确保 API 调用发生在 API 提供的信息对模型实际有帮助的位置附近。阈值 τs 和 τf 为每个工具单独选择，以确保足够数量的示例；详见附录 A。表 2 显示了我们的最终数据集（用 API 调用增强）的相关统计数据。

items.en: We finetune M𝑀M on 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} using a batch size of 128 and a learning rate of 1⋅10−5⋅1superscript1051\cdot 10^{-5} with linear warmup for the first 10% of training. Details of our finetuning procedure are given in Appendix B.
items.zh: 我们在 C∗ 上微调 M，使用批次大小 128 和学习率 1⋅10−5，并在训练的前 10% 进行线性预热。我们的微调过程细节在附录 B 中给出。

items.en: Throughout the remainder of this section, we mainly compare the following models:
items.zh: 在本节的其余部分，我们主要比较以下模型：

items.en: GPT-J: A regular GPT-J model without any finetuning.
items.zh: GPT-J：未经任何微调的常规 GPT-J 模型。

items.en: GPT-J + CC: GPT-J finetuned on 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C}, our subset of CCNet without any API calls.
items.zh: GPT-J + CC：在 C 上微调的 GPT-J，C 是我们的 CCNet 子集，不包含任何 API 调用。

items.en: Toolformer: GPT-J finetuned on 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*}, our subset of CCNet augmented with API calls.
items.zh: Toolformer：在 C∗ 上微调的 GPT-J，C∗ 是我们的 CCNet 子集，用 API 调用增强。

items.en: Toolformer (disabled): The same model as Toolformer, but API calls are disabled during decoding.555This is achieved by manually setting the probability of the <API> token to 0.
items.zh: Toolformer（禁用）：与 Toolformer 相同的模型，但在解码期间禁用 API 调用。555这是通过手动将 <API> 词元的概率设置为 0 来实现的。

items.en: For most tasks, we additionally compare to OPT (66B) (Zhang et al., 2022) and GPT-3666We use the original davinci variant that is not finetuned on any instructions. (175B) (Brown et al., 2020), two models that are about 10 and 25 times larger than our other baseline models, respectively.
items.zh: 对于大多数任务，我们还与 OPT（66B）（Zhang 等人，2022）和 GPT-3666我们使用原始的 davinci 变体，未在任何指令上进行微调。（175B）（Brown 等人，2020）进行比较，这两个模型分别比我们的其他基线模型大约 10 倍和 25 倍。

items.en: We evaluate all models on a variety of downstream tasks. In all cases, we consider a prompted zero-shot setup – i.e., models are instructed to solve each task in natural language, but we do not provide any in-context examples. This is in contrast to prior work on tool use (e.g., Gao et al., 2022; Parisi et al., 2022), where models are provided with dataset-specific examples of how a tool can be used to solve a concrete task. We choose the more challenging zero-shot setup as we are interested in seeing whether Toolformer works in precisely those cases where a user does not specify in advance which tools should be used in which way for solving a specific problem.
items.zh: 我们在多种下游任务上评估所有模型。所有情况下，我们采用提示零样本设置——即模型被指示用自然语言解决每个任务，但我们不提供任何上下文示例。这与先前关于工具使用的工作（例如，Gao等人，2022；Parisi等人，2022）形成对比，后者为模型提供了数据集特定的示例，说明如何使用工具解决具体任务。我们选择更具挑战性的零样本设置，因为我们感兴趣的是观察Toolformer是否能在用户未预先指定哪些工具应以何种方式用于解决特定问题的情况下发挥作用。

items.en: We use standard greedy decoding, but with one modification for Toolformer: We let the model start an API call not just when <API> is the most likely token, but whenever it is one of the k𝑘k most likely tokens. For k=1𝑘1k=1, this corresponds to regular greedy decoding; we instead use k=10𝑘10k=10 to increase the disposition of our model to make use of the APIs that it has access to. At the same time, we only at most one API call per input to make sure the model does not get stuck in a loop where it constantly calls APIs without producing any actual output. The effect of these modifications is explored in Section 5.
items.zh: 我们使用标准贪心解码，但对Toolformer做了一项修改：我们不仅允许模型在<API>是最可能词元时启动API调用，而且只要它是k个最可能词元之一就启动。对于k=1，这对应于常规贪心解码；我们改用k=10以增加模型使用其可访问API的倾向。同时，我们每个输入最多只允许一次API调用，以确保模型不会陷入不断调用API而不产生实际输出的循环。这些修改的效果在第5节中探讨。

items.en: We evaluate our models on the SQuAD, Google-RE and T-REx subsets of the LAMA benchmark (Petroni et al., 2019). For each of these subsets, the task is to complete a short statement with a missing fact (e.g., a date or a place). As LAMA was originally designed to evaluate masked language models (e.g., Devlin et al., 2019), we filter out examples where the mask token is not the final token, so that the remaining examples can be processed in a left-to-right fashion. To account for different tokenizations and added complexity from not informing the model that a single word is required, we use a slightly more lenient evaluation criterion than exact match and simply check whether the correct word is within the first five words predicted by the model. As LAMA is based on statements obtained directly from Wikipedia, we prevent Toolformer from using the Wikipedia Search API to avoid giving it an unfair advantage.
items.zh: 我们在LAMA基准测试（Petroni等人，2019）的SQuAD、Google-RE和T-REx子集上评估模型。对于每个子集，任务是补全一个缺少事实的简短陈述（例如，日期或地点）。由于LAMA最初设计用于评估掩码语言模型（例如，Devlin等人，2019），我们过滤掉掩码词元不是最终词元的示例，以便剩余示例可以按从左到右的方式处理。为了考虑不同的分词方式以及未告知模型需要单个词而增加的复杂性，我们采用比精确匹配稍宽松的评估标准，仅检查正确单词是否出现在模型预测的前五个单词中。由于LAMA基于直接从维基百科获取的陈述，我们阻止Toolformer使用维基百科搜索API，以避免赋予其不公平的优势。

items.en: Results for all models can be seen in Table 3. All GPT-J models without tool use achieve similar performance. Crucially, Toolformer clearly outperforms these baseline models, improving upon the best baseline by 11.7, 5.2 and 18.6 points, respectively. It also clearly outperforms OPT (66B) and GPT-3 (175B), despite both models being much larger. This is achieved because the model independently decides to ask the question answering tool for the required information in almost all cases (98.1%); for only very few examples, it uses a different tool (0.7%) or no tool at all (1.2%).
items.zh: 所有模型的结果见表3。所有不使用工具的GPT-J模型表现相似。关键的是，Toolformer明显优于这些基线模型，分别比最佳基线提高了11.7、5.2和18.6个百分点。它还明显优于OPT（66B）和GPT-3（175B），尽管这两个模型大得多。这是因为模型在几乎所有情况下（98.1%）独立决定使用问答工具获取所需信息；只有极少数示例使用其他工具（0.7%）或根本不使用工具（1.2%）。

items.en: We test mathematical reasoning abilities on ASDiv (Miao et al., 2020), SVAMP (Patel et al., 2021) and the MAWPS benchmark (Koncel-Kedziorski et al., 2016). We again account for the fact that we test all models in a zero-shot setup by using a more lenient evaluation criterion: As the required output is always a number, we simply check for the first number predicted by the model.777An exception to this is if the model’s prediction contains an equation (e.g., “The correct answer is 5+3=8”), in which case we consider the first number after the “=” sign to be its prediction.
items.zh: 我们在ASDiv（Miao等人，2020）、SVAMP（Patel等人，2021）和MAWPS基准测试（Koncel-Kedziorski等人，2016）上测试数学推理能力。我们再次考虑到在零样本设置下测试所有模型，因此采用更宽松的评估标准：由于所需输出始终是数字，我们仅检查模型预测的第一个数字。例外情况是，如果模型的预测包含方程（例如，“正确答案是5+3=8”），则我们将“=”号后的第一个数字视为其预测。

items.en: We look at Web Questions (Berant et al., 2013), Natural Questions (Kwiatkowski et al., 2019) and TriviaQA (Joshi et al., 2017), the three question answering datasets considered by Brown et al. (2020). For evaluation, we check whether the first 20 words predicted by a model contain the correct answer instead of requiring an exact match. For Toolformer, we disable the question answering tool as this would make solving the tasks trivial, especially given that the underlying QA system was finetuned on Natural Questions.
items.zh: 我们考察了Web Questions（Berant等人，2013）、Natural Questions（Kwiatkowski等人，2019）和TriviaQA（Joshi等人，2017），这是Brown等人（2020）考虑的三个问答数据集。评估时，我们检查模型预测的前20个单词是否包含正确答案，而不是要求精确匹配。对于Toolformer，我们禁用问答工具，因为这将使任务变得简单，尤其是考虑到底层QA系统是在Natural Questions上微调的。

items.en: Results are shown in Table 5. Once again, Toolformer clearly outperforms all other models based on GPT-J, this time mostly relying on the Wikipedia search API (99.3%) to find relevant information. However, Toolformer still lags behind the much larger GPT-3 (175B) model. This is likely due to both the simplicity of our search engine (in many cases, it returns results that are clearly not a good match for a given query) and the inability of Toolformer to interact with it, e.g., by reformulating its query if results are not helpful or by browsing through multiple of the top results. We believe that adding this functionality is an exciting direction for future work.
items.zh: 结果见表5。再次，Toolformer明显优于所有基于GPT-J的其他模型，这次主要依赖维基百科搜索API（99.3%）来查找相关信息。然而，Toolformer仍然落后于更大的GPT-3（175B）模型。这可能是由于我们搜索引擎的简单性（在许多情况下，它返回的结果明显与给定查询不匹配）以及Toolformer无法与之交互，例如，如果结果无帮助则重新表述查询，或浏览多个顶部结果。我们认为添加此功能是未来工作的一个令人兴奋的方向。

items.en: We evaluate Toolformer and all baseline models on MLQA (Lewis et al., 2019), a multilingual question-answering benchmark. A context paragraph for each question is provided in English, while the question can be in Arabic, German, Spanish, Hindi, Vietnamese, or Simplified Chinese. In order to solve the task, the model needs to be able to understand both the paragraph and the question, so it may benefit from translating the question into English. Our evaluation metric is the percentage of times the model’s generation, capped at 10 words, contains the correct answer.
items.zh: 我们在MLQA（Lewis等人，2019）上评估Toolformer和所有基线模型，这是一个多语言问答基准测试。每个问题的上下文段落以英文提供，而问题可以是阿拉伯语、德语、西班牙语、印地语、越南语或简体中文。为了解决任务，模型需要理解段落和问题，因此可能受益于将问题翻译成英文。我们的评估指标是模型生成（限制为10个单词）包含正确答案的百分比。

items.en: Results are shown in Table 6. Using API calls consistently improves Toolformer’s performance for all languages, suggesting that it has learned to make use of the machine translation tool. Depending on the language, this tool is used for 63.8% to 94.9% of all examples; the only exception to this is Hindi, for which the machine translation tool is used in only 7.3% of cases. However, Toolformer does not consistently outperform vanilla GPT-J. This is mainly because for some languages, finetuning on CCNet deteriorates performance; this might be due to a distribution shift compared to GPT-J’s original pretraining data.
items.zh: 结果见表6。使用API调用持续提高了Toolformer在所有语言上的性能，表明它已学会使用机器翻译工具。根据语言不同，该工具用于63.8%到94.9%的示例；唯一的例外是印地语，机器翻译工具仅用于7.3%的情况。然而，Toolformer并不始终优于原始GPT-J。这主要是因为对于某些语言，在CCNet上微调会降低性能；这可能是由于与GPT-J原始预训练数据相比存在分布偏移。

items.en: OPT and GPT-3 perform surprisingly weak across all languages, mostly because they fail to provide an answer in English despite being instructed to do so. A potential reason for GPT-J not suffering from this problem is that it was trained on more multilingual data than both OPT and GPT-3, including the EuroParl corpus (Koehn, 2005; Gao et al., 2020). As an upper bound, we also evaluate GPT-J and GPT-3 on a variant of MLQA where both the context and the question are provided in English. In this setup, GPT-3 performs better than all other models, supporting our hypothesis that its subpar performance on MLQA is due to the multilingual aspect of the task.
items.zh: OPT和GPT-3在所有语言上的表现出奇地弱，主要是因为尽管被指示用英文回答，但它们未能提供英文答案。GPT-J没有出现此问题的一个可能原因是，它比OPT和GPT-3在更多多语言数据上训练，包括EuroParl语料库（Koehn，2005；Gao等人，2020）。作为上限，我们还在上下文和问题均为英文的MLQA变体上评估了GPT-J和GPT-3。在这种设置下，GPT-3优于所有其他模型，支持了我们的假设，即其在MLQA上表现不佳是由于任务的多语言方面。

items.en: To investigate the calendar API’s utility, we evaluate all models on TempLAMA (Dhingra et al., 2022) and a new dataset that we call Dateset. TempLAMA is a dataset built from Wikidata that contains cloze queries about facts that change with time (e.g., “Cristiano Ronaldo plays for ___”) as well as the correct answer for the years between 2010 and 2020. Dateset, described in Appendix D, is also generated through a series of templates, but populated using a combination of random dates/durations (e.g., “What day of the week was it 30 days ago?”). Critically, knowing the current date is required to answer these questions. For both tasks, we use the same evaluation as for the original LAMA dataset.
items.zh: 为了研究日历API的效用，我们在TempLAMA（Dhingra等人，2022）和一个我们称为Dateset的新数据集上评估所有模型。TempLAMA是一个基于Wikidata构建的数据集，包含关于随时间变化的事实（例如，“克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多为___效力”）的完形查询，以及2010年至2020年间的正确答案。Dateset（附录D中描述）也通过一系列模板生成，但使用随机日期/持续时间填充（例如，“30天前是星期几？”）。关键的是，回答这些问题需要知道当前日期。对于这两个任务，我们使用与原始LAMA数据集相同的评估方法。

items.en: Results shown in Table 7 illustrate that Toolformer outperforms all baselines for both TempLAMA and Dateset. However, closer inspection shows that improvements on TempLAMA can not be attributed to the calendar tool, which is only used for 0.2% of all examples, but mostly to the Wikipedia search and question answering tools, which Toolformer calls the most. This makes sense given that named entities in TempLama are often so specific and rare that even knowing the exact date alone would be of little help. The best course of action for this dataset – first querying the calendar API to get the current date, and then querying the question answering system with this date – is not only prohibited by our restriction of using at most one API call per example, but also hard to learn for Toolformer given that all API calls in its training data are sampled independently.
items.zh: 表7所示的结果表明，Toolformer在TempLAMA和Dateset上均优于所有基线。然而，仔细检查发现，TempLAMA上的改进不能归因于日历工具（仅用于0.2%的示例），而主要归因于Toolformer最常调用的维基百科搜索和问答工具。这是合理的，因为TempLAMA中的命名实体通常非常具体和罕见，即使仅知道确切日期也帮助不大。对于该数据集的最佳行动方案——首先查询日历API获取当前日期，然后使用此日期查询问答系统——不仅受到我们每个示例最多使用一个API调用的限制，而且由于训练数据中所有API调用都是独立采样的，Toolformer难以学习。

items.en: For Dateset, on the other hand, the considerable improvement of Toolformer compared to other models can be fully accredited to the calendar tool, which it makes use of for 54.8% of all examples.
items.zh: 另一方面，对于Dateset，Toolformer相比其他模型的显著改进可以完全归功于日历工具，它在54.8%的示例中使用了该工具。

items.en: In addition to verifying improved performance on various downstream tasks, we also want to ensure that language modeling performance of Toolformer does not degrade through our finetuning with API calls. To this end, we evaluate our models on two language modeling datasets: WikiText (Merity et al., 2017) and a subset of 10,000 randomly selected documents from CCNet (Wenzek et al., 2020) that were not used during training. Perplexities of various models are shown in Table 8. As one would expect, finetuning on CCNet leads to slightly improved performance on a different CCNet subset, but it slightly deteriorates performance on WikiText, presumably because the original pretraining data for GPT-J is more similar to WikiText than our randomly selected subset of CCNet. Most importantly, however, training on 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} (our dataset annotated with API calls) does not lead to an increase in perplexity compared to training on 𝒞𝒞\mathcal{C} when API calls are disabled at inference time.888We do not evaluate the perplexity of Toolformer with API calls enabled as computing the probability pM​(xt∣x1,…,xt−1)subscript𝑝𝑀conditionalsubscript𝑥𝑡subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑡1p_{M}(x_{t}\mid x_{1},\ldots,x_{t-1}) of token xtsubscript𝑥𝑡x_{t} given x1,…,xt−1subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑡1x_{1},\ldots,x_{t-1} would require marginalizing over all potential API calls that the model could make at position t𝑡t, which is intractable.
items.zh: 除了验证在多种下游任务上的性能提升外，我们还希望确保Toolformer的语言建模性能不会因使用API调用的微调而下降。为此，我们在两个语言建模数据集上评估模型：WikiText（Merity等人，2017）和来自CCNet（Wenzek等人，2020）的10,000个随机选择文档的子集（未在训练中使用）。各种模型的困惑度见表8。正如预期，在CCNet上微调导致在另一个CCNet子集上性能略有提升，但在WikiText上性能略有下降，可能是因为GPT-J的原始预训练数据比我们随机选择的CCNet子集更接近WikiText。然而，最重要的是，在推理时禁用API调用的情况下，在C*（我们的带API调用注释的数据集）上训练与在C上训练相比，不会导致困惑度增加。我们不在启用API调用时评估Toolformer的困惑度，因为计算给定x1,...,x_{t-1}下词元x_t的概率p_M(x_t|x_1,...,x_{t-1})需要对模型在位置t可能做出的所有潜在API调用进行边缘化，这是难以处理的。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Average performance on LAMA, our math benchmarks and our QA benchmarks for GPT-2 models of different sizes and GPT-J finetuned with our approach, both with and without API calls. While API calls are not helpful to the smallest models, larger models learn how to make good use of them. Even for bigger models, the gap between model predictions with and without API calls remains high.
items.capZh: 图4：不同大小的GPT-2模型和GPT-J（使用我们的方法微调）在LAMA、数学基准和问答基准上的平均性能，包括有和没有API调用的情况。虽然API调用对最小的模型没有帮助，但较大的模型学会了如何很好地利用它们。即使对于更大的模型，有和没有API调用的模型预测之间的差距仍然很大。

items.en: We investigate how the ability to ask external tools for help affects performance as we vary the size of our LM. To this end, we apply our approach not just to GPT-J, but also to four smaller models from the GPT-2 family (Radford et al., 2019), with 124M, 355M, 775M and 1.6B parameters, respectively. We do so using only a subset of three tools: the question answering system, the calculator, and the Wikipedia search engine. Apart from this, we follow the experimental setup described in Section 4.1.
items.zh: 我们研究了向外部工具求助的能力如何随LM大小的变化而影响性能。为此，我们将方法不仅应用于GPT-J，还应用于GPT-2系列（Radford等人，2019）的四个较小模型，参数分别为124M、355M、775M和1.6B。我们仅使用三个工具的子集：问答系统、计算器和维基百科搜索引擎。除此之外，我们遵循第4.1节中描述的实验设置。

items.en: We investigate the effect of our modified decoding strategy introduced in Section 4.2, where instead of always generating the most likely token, we generate the <API> token if it is one of the k𝑘k most likely tokens. Table 9 shows performance on the T-REx subset of LAMA and on WebQS for different values of k𝑘k. As expected, increasing k𝑘k leads to the model doing API calls for more examples – from 40.3% and 8.5% with k=1𝑘1k=1 (i.e., regular greedy decoding) to 98.1% and 100% for k=10𝑘10k=10. While for T-REx, there is already a clear improvement in performance with greedy decoding, on WebQS our model only starts to make a substantial number of API calls as we slightly increase k𝑘k. Interestingly, for k=1𝑘1k=1 the model is calibrated to some extent: It decides to call APIs for examples that it would perform particularly badly on without making API calls. This can be seen from the fact that performance on examples where it decides not to make an API call (44.3 and 19.9) is higher than average performance if no API calls are made at all (34.9 and 18.9). However, this calibration is lost for higher values of k𝑘k.
items.zh: 我们研究了第4.2节中引入的修改解码策略的效果，即不是总是生成最可能的词元，而是如果<API>是k个最可能词元之一则生成它。表9显示了对于不同的k值，在LAMA的T-REx子集和WebQS上的性能。正如预期，增加k导致模型对更多示例进行API调用——从k=1（即常规贪心解码）时的40.3%和8.5%增加到k=10时的98.1%和100%。对于T-REx，贪心解码时性能已有明显提升，而在WebQS上，模型仅在稍微增加k时才开始进行大量API调用。有趣的是，对于k=1，模型在一定程度上是校准的：它决定对如果不进行API调用就会表现特别差的示例调用API。这可以从以下事实看出：它决定不进行API调用的示例上的性能（44.3和19.9）高于完全不进行API调用时的平均性能（34.9和18.9）。然而，对于更高的k值，这种校准会丢失。

items.en: We qualitatively analyze some API calls generated with our approach for different APIs. Table 10 shows some examples of texts from CCNet augmented with API calls, as well as the corresponding score Li−−Li+superscriptsubscript𝐿𝑖superscriptsubscript𝐿𝑖L_{i}^{-}-L_{i}^{+} that is used as a filtering criterion, and whether the API calls made by the model are intuitively useful in the given context. As can be seen, high values of Li−−Li+superscriptsubscript𝐿𝑖superscriptsubscript𝐿𝑖L_{i}^{-}-L_{i}^{+} typically correspond to useful API calls, whereas low values correspond to API calls that do not provide any information that is useful for predicting future tokens. There are some exceptions, e.g., an API call for “Fast train success” in the fourth example that does not give any relevant information but still reduces perplexity. However, some amount of noise in the API calls that are not filtered can actually be useful as it forces the model finetuned on 𝒞∗superscript𝒞\mathcal{C}^{*} to not always blindly follow the results of each call it makes.
items.zh: 我们定性分析了一些使用我们的方法为不同API生成的API调用。表10显示了来自CCNet的一些文本示例，这些文本通过API调用进行了增强，以及用作过滤标准的相应分数L_i^- - L_i^+，以及模型进行的API调用在给定上下文中是否直观有用。可以看出，L_i^- - L_i^+的高值通常对应于有用的API调用，而低值对应于不提供任何有助于预测未来词元信息的API调用。有一些例外，例如第四个示例中“Fast train success”的API调用没有提供任何相关信息，但仍然降低了困惑度。然而，未过滤的API调用中的一些噪声实际上可能是有用的，因为它迫使在C*上微调的模型不要总是盲目遵循其每次调用的结果。

items.en: There are various approaches that augment language models with some form of additional textual information during pretraining, including various forms of metadata (Keskar et al., 2019), HTML tags (Aghajanyan et al., 2021), Wikipedia markup (Schick et al., 2022), or related texts obtained from an information retrieval system (Guu et al., 2020; Borgeaud et al., 2021; Izacard et al., 2022). For all of these approaches, additional information is always provided, regardless of whether it is helpful or not. In contrast, Toolformer learns for itself to explicitly asks for the right information.
items.zh: 有各种方法在预训练期间用某种形式的额外文本信息增强语言模型，包括各种形式的元数据（Keskar等人，2019）、HTML标签（Aghajanyan等人，2021）、维基百科标记（Schick等人，2022）或从信息检索系统获得的相关文本（Guu等人，2020；Borgeaud等人，2021；Izacard等人，2022）。对于所有这些方法，无论是否有帮助，都会提供额外信息。相比之下，Toolformer自己学会明确请求正确的信息。

items.en: Several approaches aim to equip LMs with the ability to use external tools such as search engines (Komeili et al., 2022; Thoppilan et al., 2022; Lazaridou et al., 2022; Shuster et al., 2022; Yao et al., 2022), web browsers (Nakano et al., 2021), calculators (Cobbe et al., 2021; Thoppilan et al., 2022), translation systems (Thoppilan et al., 2022) and Python interpreters (Gao et al., 2022). The way these models learn to use tools can roughly be divided into two approaches: Either they rely on large amounts of human supervision (Komeili et al., 2022; Nakano et al., 2021; Thoppilan et al., 2022) or they work by prompting the language model in a few-shot setup tailored towards a specific task where it is known a priori which tools needs to be used (Gao et al., 2022; Lazaridou et al., 2022; Yao et al., 2022). In contrast, the self-supervised nature of Toolformer enables it to learn how and when to use tools without requiring a specific prompt that shows task-specific examples of how a tool could be used. Perhaps most closely related to our work is TALM (Parisi et al., 2022), an approach that uses a similar self-supervised objective for teaching a model to use a calculator and a search engine, but explores this only in settings where a model is finetuned for downstream tasks.
items.zh: 有几种方法旨在使LM具备使用外部工具的能力，例如搜索引擎（Komeili等人，2022；Thoppilan等人，2022；Lazaridou等人，2022；Shuster等人，2022；Yao等人，2022）、网络浏览器（Nakano等人，2021）、计算器（Cobbe等人，2021；Thoppilan等人，2022）、翻译系统（Thoppilan等人，2022）和Python解释器（Gao等人，2022）。这些模型学习使用工具的方式大致可分为两种方法：要么依赖大量人工监督（Komeili等人，2022；Nakano等人，2021；Thoppilan等人，2022），要么通过针对特定任务定制的少样本设置提示语言模型，其中预先知道需要哪些工具（Gao等人，2022；Lazaridou等人，2022；Yao等人，2022）。相比之下，Toolformer的自监督性质使其能够学习如何以及何时使用工具，而无需显示任务特定示例的特定提示。与我们的工作最接近的可能是TALM（Parisi等人，2022），该方法使用类似的自监督目标来教模型使用计算器和搜索引擎，但仅在模型为下游任务微调的情况下进行探索。

items.en: The idea of using self-training and bootstrapping techniques to improve models has been investigated in various contexts, ranging from word sense disambiguation (Yarowsky, 1995), relation extraction (Brin, 1999; Agichtein and Gravano, 2000), parsing (McClosky et al., 2006; Reichart and Rappoport, 2007), sequence generation (He et al., 2020), few-shot text classification (Schick and Schütze, 2021a) and retrieval (Izacard and Grave, 2021) to reasoning (Zelikman et al., 2022). In a similar spirit to these approaches, Toolformer is trained on its own predictions after applying a perplexity-based filtering step.
items.zh: 使用自训练和自举技术改进模型的想法已在各种背景下得到研究，从词义消歧（Yarowsky, 1995）、关系抽取（Brin, 1999; Agichtein and Gravano, 2000）、句法分析（McClosky et al., 2006; Reichart and Rappoport, 2007）、序列生成（He et al., 2020）、少样本文本分类（Schick and Schütze, 2021a）和检索（Izacard and Grave, 2021）到推理（Zelikman et al., 2022）。与这些方法类似，Toolformer 在应用基于困惑度的过滤步骤后，在其自身预测上进行训练。

items.en: While our approach enables LMs to learn how to use a variety of tools in a self-supervised way, there are some clear limitations to what can be achieved with our method in its current form. One such limitation is the inability of Toolformer to use tools in a chain (i.e., using the output of one tool as an input for another tool). This is due to the fact that API calls for each tool are generated independently; as a consequence, there are no examples of chained tool use in the finetuning dataset. Our current approach also does not allow the LM to use a tool in an interactive way – especially for tools such as search engines, that could potentially return hundreds of different results, enabling a LM to browse through these results or to refine its search query in a similar spirit to Nakano et al. (2021) can be crucial for certain applications. Beyond this, we found models trained with Toolformer to often be sensitive to the exact wording of their input when deciding whether or not to call an API; this is perhaps unsurprising given that LMs are known to be very sensitive to the prompt they are provided with in both zero-and few-shot settings (Jiang et al., 2020; Schick and Schütze, 2021a). Depending on the tool, our method is also very sample-inefficient; for example, processing more than a million documents results in only a few thousand examples of useful calls to the calculator API. A potential solution to this problem might be to iteratively apply our approach, similar to how this is done in related bootstrapping approaches (Schick and Schütze, 2021a; Izacard and Grave, 2021; Parisi et al., 2022). Finally, when deciding whether or not to make an API call, Toolformer currently does not take into account the tool-dependent, computational cost incurred from making an API call.
items.zh: 虽然我们的方法使语言模型能够以自监督方式学习使用多种工具，但当前形式的方法所能实现的目标存在一些明显局限。一个局限是 Toolformer 无法链式使用工具（即，将一个工具的输出作为另一个工具的输入）。这是因为每个工具的 API 调用是独立生成的；因此，微调数据集中没有链式工具使用的示例。我们当前的方法也不允许语言模型以交互方式使用工具——特别是对于搜索引擎等工具，它们可能返回数百个不同结果，使语言模型能够浏览这些结果或优化其搜索查询（类似于 Nakano et al. (2021)）对于某些应用可能至关重要。除此之外，我们发现使用 Toolformer 训练的模型在决定是否调用 API 时，往往对其输入的具体措辞敏感；这或许并不令人意外，因为已知语言模型在零样本和少样本设置中对提供的提示非常敏感（Jiang et al., 2020; Schick and Schütze, 2021a）。根据工具的不同，我们的方法也非常样本低效；例如，处理超过一百万份文档仅产生几千个有用的计算器 API 调用示例。解决此问题的一个潜在方法是迭代应用我们的方法，类似于相关自举方法中的做法（Schick and Schütze, 2021a; Izacard and Grave, 2021; Parisi et al., 2022）。最后，在决定是否进行 API 调用时，Toolformer 当前未考虑因 API 调用而产生的、依赖于工具的计算成本。

items.en: We have introduced Toolformer, a language model that learns in a self-supervised way how to use different tools such as search engines, calculators, and translation systems via simple API calls. This is done by finetuning on a large number of sampled API calls that are filtered based on whether they reduce perplexity on future tokens. Toolformer considerably improves zero-shot performance of a 6.7B parameter GPT-J model, enabling it to even outperform a much larger GPT-3 model on a range of different downstream tasks.
items.zh: 我们介绍了 Toolformer，一种语言模型，它以自监督方式学习如何通过简单的 API 调用使用不同工具，如搜索引擎、计算器和翻译系统。这是通过微调大量采样的 API 调用实现的，这些调用基于它们是否降低未来词元的困惑度进行过滤。Toolformer 显著提高了 6.7B 参数 GPT-J 模型的零样本性能，使其甚至能够在各种不同下游任务上超越更大的 GPT-3 模型。
